skill.name: dispatching-parallel-agents
skill.description: Guides AI agents to split independent coding failures into scoped parallel subagent tasks, then review and integrate the results.
skill.summary: Coordinate parallel subagents for independent coding fixes.
skill.author: CodingCossack

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged command execution, weak cryptography, and network reconnaissance patterns, but the cited content is documentation text and fenced examples. No executable scripts, network calls, credential access, prompt injection, or malicious intent were found in SKILL.md.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Fenced Examples Marked as Command Execution
low_findings.description: The static analyzer flagged multiple fenced blocks as Ruby or shell backtick execution. The cited lines contain a checklist, TypeScript-style Task examples, a markdown prompt template, and agent labels, not executable shell commands.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited regions are clearly fenced documentation examples. They do not define scripts, invoke a shell, or interpolate user input into commands.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptography Pattern
low_findings.description: The static analyzer reported weak cryptography on the description line. The line only describes dispatching subagents for independent failures and contains no hash, cipher, password, token, or cryptographic API.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Direct inspection shows no cryptographic operation or weak algorithm reference. This is a pattern mismatch on front matter text.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Network Reconnaissance Pattern
low_findings.description: The static analyzer reported network reconnaissance on a line that says each agent needs a scope. The line does not mention scanning, ports, hosts, IP ranges, or network tools.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited line is plain workflow guidance for scoping a task. No network activity or reconnaissance intent is present.

low_findings.title: Operational Risk: Parallel Agents May Edit Overlapping Files
low_findings.description: The skill delegates independent work to multiple agents, which can create conflicts if task boundaries are wrong. The instructions mitigate this by requiring one owner per file, conflict checks, and a full test suite before completion.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Parallel editing is an inherent workflow risk, but the skill explicitly describes review and conflict handling. This supports a low operational risk rather than a security block.

content.value_statement: Complex repair work slows down when unrelated failures are handled one at a time. This skill helps Claude, Codex, and Claude Code split independent coding tasks across focused agents.
actual_capabilities: Identifies when failures belong to independent files or subsystems.
Creates focused subagent tasks with scope, goal, constraints, and expected output.
Shows how to dispatch multiple agent tasks in parallel.
Defines review steps for summaries, file conflicts, and full test results.
Provides a reusable prompt template for failing test investigations.
limitations: It does not verify that domains are truly independent.
It does not run tests or inspect repository state by itself.
It requires the user or host agent to integrate all subagent results.
It is not suitable when failures share state or ordering dependencies.

use_cases.title: Split Failing Test Files
use_cases.description: Assign each independent failing test file to a separate agent and combine the fixes after review.

use_cases.title: Coordinate Refactor Cleanup
use_cases.description: Divide unrelated post-refactor regressions by subsystem so each agent can investigate one bounded area.

use_cases.title: Manage Multi-Agent Repair Work
use_cases.description: Create clear agent prompts and integration checks for teams using AI coding assistants.

prompt_templates.title: Find Independent Domains
prompt_templates.prompt: Review these failures and group them by independent file or subsystem. Tell me which groups can be handled in parallel and which must stay together.

prompt_templates.title: Draft Agent Tasks
prompt_templates.scenario: Create clear prompts for several subagents.
prompt_templates.prompt: Turn these independent failure groups into focused agent tasks. Include scope, goal, constraints, relevant context, and the expected summary for each agent.

prompt_templates.title: Dispatch and Track Work
prompt_templates.scenario: Run a structured parallel repair workflow.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the dispatching-parallel-agents workflow for these unrelated failures. Create the checklist, assign one agent per domain, and track review steps before integration.

prompt_templates.title: Review Agent Outputs
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced integration after multiple agents finish.
prompt_templates.prompt: Review these agent summaries for file conflicts, shared assumptions, missed dependencies, and test coverage gaps. Recommend the safest integration order.

output_examples.input: Three test files fail after a refactor, and each failure concerns a different subsystem.
output_examples.output: The skill groups the failures by subsystem, creates three focused agent tasks, and requires conflict checks before the final test run.

output_examples.input: Two agents report fixes in the same shared utility file.
output_examples.output: The skill directs the host agent to stop integration, re-scope ownership, inspect the conflict, and rerun the full suite after resolution.

output_examples.input: A user asks one agent to fix all failures at once.
output_examples.output: The skill narrows the work into independent domains and asks for test names, error summaries, constraints, and expected return summaries.

best_practices: Dispatch agents only after confirming the failures are independent.
Give each agent one file or subsystem owner and clear constraints.
Run the full test suite after integrating all agent changes.
anti_patterns: Do not parallelize failures that may share one root cause.
Do not assign broad tasks such as fixing all tests.
Do not merge agent changes without checking touched files and summaries.

faq.question: When should I use this skill?
faq.answer: Use it when two or more unrelated coding failures can be investigated without shared state or ordering dependencies.

faq.question: Can it decide independence automatically?
faq.answer: No. It gives a decision framework, but the host agent or user must confirm independence.

faq.question: Does it run subagents by itself?
faq.answer: No. It provides the workflow and prompt structure for tools that support subagent dispatch.

faq.question: What is the main safety check?
faq.answer: Each agent should own one file or subsystem, and the host agent should check for overlapping edits before integration.

faq.question: Is this useful outside test failures?
faq.answer: Yes. It can help with independent bugs, subsystems, or cleanup tasks when the boundaries are clear.

faq.question: Why is the marketplace risk low?
faq.answer: The skill contains workflow documentation only. The only concern is coordination risk from parallel edits, which the instructions address.
